enriched_points.expanded_content: Individual motivations, experiences, and biases of citizen scientists can influence their data collection methods, leading to inconsistencies in the recorded observations.
Data collected opportunistically may not represent true ecological patterns, resulting in skewed results that misinform biodiversity assessments and conservation efforts.
Sampling gaps and biases undermine the integrity of collected data, complicating analyses and potentially leading to flawed conclusions about species distributions.
